title: Measurement & shape
subtitle: Area, volume, units and scale questions from real-life contexts.
curriculumLinks: MCS.3: area and perimeter of 2D shapes
MCS.3: volume and surface area of 3D shapes

diagrams.title: Common shape formulas
diagrams.description: Pick the formula that matches the shape in the question.

diagrams.title: Scale model thinking
diagrams.description: A scale of 1:12 means every 1 cm on the model is 12 cm in real life.

rules.title: Area measures flat space
rules.explanation: Area is the amount of surface covered. A rectangle uses length times width. A circle uses pi times radius squared.
rules.formula: rectangle area = length x width; circle area = pi x r x r
steps: Check all measurements use the same unit.
Write square units such as m2 or cm2.

examples.question: A bedroom floor is 6 m long and 4 m wide.

rules.title: Volume measures space inside
rules.explanation: Volume is used for boxes, tanks and containers. A rectangular solid uses length times width times height.
rules.formula: cuboid volume = length x width x height
steps: Write cubic units such as cm3 or m3.

examples.question: A box is 10 cm by 5 cm by 4 cm.

rules.title: Cylinder volume uses the circular base
rules.explanation: A cylinder is like a circle stretched upwards. Find the area of the circle, then multiply by height.
rules.formula: cylinder volume = pi x r x r x h
steps: Use the radius, not the diameter.
Multiply pi by radius by radius by height.
Divide by 1000 to change cm3 into litres.

examples.question: A cylinder has radius 5 cm and height 10 cm.

rules.title: Scale connects model and real size
rules.explanation: A scale such as 1:12 means 1 unit on the model represents 12 of the same units in real life.
rules.formula: real length = model length x scale factor
steps: Multiply model size by the scale factor for real size.
Convert units if the answer needs metres or kilometres.

examples.question: A model car is 35 cm long at a scale of 1:12.
